summary: Cleaned up Zelda fighter motion-state definitions by replacing raw motion flag and move-id hex constants in `ftZd_Init_MotionStateTable` with named `ftZd_MotionFlags` enum values and `FtMoveId_* << 24` expressions. The PR also moved the Zelda-specific motion flag compositions into `ftZelda/forward.h`, added the needed `ft/forward.h` include, included `forward.h` from `ftZd_Init.c`, and converted two item IDs in `ftZd_Init_OnLoad` from hex literals `0x6CU`/`0x6DU` to decimal `108`/`109`.
what_changed: Added `#include "ft/forward.h"` to `src/melee/ft/chara/ftZelda/forward.h` so Zelda-specific motion flags can be expressed in terms of shared `FtStateChange_*` constants.

Introduced `typedef enum ftZd_MotionFlags` containing composed Zelda motion flag groups: `ftZd_MF_Special`, `ftZd_MF_SpecialN`, `ftZd_MF_SpecialHi`, `ftZd_MF_SpecialLw`, `ftZd_MF_SpecialS`, and their aerial variants with `FtStateChange_SkipUpdateParasol`.
Added `#include "forward.h"` to `ftZd_Init.c` to make the new `ftZd_MotionFlags` names available.

Replaced raw first flag fields in `ftZd_Init_MotionStateTable`, such as `0x00341011`, `0x00341411`, `0x00340112`, `0x00340512`, `0x00340013`, `0x00340413`, `0x00340014`, and `0x00340414`, with named `ftZd_MF_*` constants.

Replaced raw move-id fields such as `0x12000000`, `0x13000000`, `0x14000000`, and `0x15000000` with `FtMoveId_SpecialN << 24`, `FtMoveId_SpecialS << 24`, `FtMoveId_SpecialHi << 24`, and `FtMoveId_SpecialLw << 24`.
Changed two `it_8026B3F8` calls in `ftZd_Init_OnLoad` from unsigned hex item IDs `0x6CU` and `0x6DU` to decimal literals `108` and `109`.
smart_moves: Factored repeated flag bitmasks into layered enum aliases instead of replacing each hex literal with a long inline OR expression.
Created a base `ftZd_MF_Special` flag group for common Zelda special-state behavior, then derived ground and air variants from it.
Modeled aerial variants by OR-ing the corresponding grounded flag group with `FtStateChange_SkipUpdateParasol`, making the ground/air relationship explicit.
Used existing shared `FtMoveId_*` names shifted by 24 bits to document the semantic meaning of the second motion-state table word while preserving the original encoded value.
Placed Zelda-specific motion flag aliases in `forward.h`, making them reusable by Zelda source files without cluttering the motion-state table.
decomp_lessons: When motion-state table words are known bitfield compositions, prefer named enums built from shared `FtStateChange_*` constants over opaque hex constants.
For fighter-specific motion flags, define a small fighter-local enum with a consistent prefix rather than repeating the same OR chains in every table row.
Preserve exact table values while improving readability by expressing encoded move IDs as `FtMoveId_* << 24`.
A cleanup-only PR can still materially improve future matching and review work by making state categories, move IDs, and shared flag differences visible at a glance.

Header placement matters: fighter-local forward headers can host lightweight enums that are needed by initialization tables, provided they include the shared forward declarations/constants they depend on.
naming_conventions: Zelda fighter symbols use the `ftZd_` prefix.
Zelda motion flags were named with the pattern `ftZd_MF_<MoveOrVariant>`, e.g. `ftZd_MF_SpecialN`, `ftZd_MF_SpecialAirHi`, and `ftZd_MF_SpecialAirLw`.
Common motion-state move IDs use the existing `FtMoveId_SpecialN`, `FtMoveId_SpecialS`, `FtMoveId_SpecialHi`, and `FtMoveId_SpecialLw` names.
Shared state-change bit constants use the `FtStateChange_*` prefix, such as `FtStateChange_PreserveFastFall`, `FtStateChange_PreserveGfx`, and `FtStateChange_SkipUpdateParasol`.
Zelda action-state enum naming remains `ftZelda_AS` with values starting at `ftZd_MS_SpecialN = ftCo_MS_Count`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Kept generated constants equivalent by replacing hex flag words with OR-composed enum values that should compile to the same numeric values.
Represented high-byte move identifiers with `FtMoveId_* << 24`, preserving the encoded `0x12/0x13/0x14/0x15` high-byte layout while removing magic numbers.
The flag enum derives repeated combinations from base combinations, reducing the chance of mismatched bitfields when auditing or extending motion-state rows.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR context, so no human review feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find other fighter `*_Init_MotionStateTable` definitions still using raw motion flag hex constants.
Search for other occurrences of `FtMoveId_* << 24` to compare move-id encoding conventions across fighters.
Audit other fighter `forward.h` files for local `*_MotionFlags` enums that can be used as naming templates.
Search for remaining `0x0034` Zelda or fighter motion-state flag literals.
